raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites assessment of the contribution framing, especially the relation between the anomaly-detection claim, benchmark claim, and stated deliverable.","R1.2":"The artifact contains an argument chain from setup to method to guarantees and experiments, so checking internal consistency is a central responsibility.","R1.3":"The strongest responsibility is to assess whether claims such as unsupervised detection, formal guarantees, and empirical performance are supported by the described method and experiments.","R1.4":"The artifact makes explicit first-work and no-prior-work claims, making novelty assessment a central part of a substantive reaction.","RX.1":"Any projection of an open-ended opinion request should preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and evaluation standard.","RX.2":"The response must guard against accepting or amplifying strong claims such as firstness, guarantees, and unsupervised status without qualification.","RX.3":"The delegation asks for an opinion on the provided artifact only, so scope adherence is always active.","RX.4":"Because the artifact concerns evaluation of LLM judges and bias diagnostics, avoiding misleading validation or harmful overconfidence is relevant.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to specific statements in the artifact, such as the threshold calibration and benchmark-deliverable claims."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the contribution framing, especially the relation between the anomaly-detection claim, benchmark claim, and stated deliverable.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains an argument chain from setup to method to guarantees and experiments, so checking internal consistency is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: The strongest responsibility is to assess whether claims such as unsupervised detection, formal guarantees, and empirical performance are supported by the described method and experiments.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes explicit first-work and no-prior-work claims, making novelty assessment a central part of a substantive reaction.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an open-ended opinion request should preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and evaluation standard.
rationale.RX.2: The response must guard against accepting or amplifying strong claims such as firstness, guarantees, and unsupervised status without qualification.
rationale.RX.3: The delegation asks for an opinion on the provided artifact only, so scope adherence is always active.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns evaluation of LLM judges and bias diagnostics, avoiding misleading validation or harmful overconfidence is relevant.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to specific statements in the artifact, such as the threshold calibration and benchmark-deliverable claims.
